title: Tempo of the Rush Hour
description: The Tempo arrives as a pizza delivery driver in a beat-up compact car with a magnetic MAPLE HEIGHTS PIZZA roof sign. Thirty minutes or less. The timer is always running.

steps.text: The compact car appeared at the end of the street doing a speed that was technically residential but felt much faster. On the roof: a magnetic sign in red and yellow. MAPLE HEIGHTS PIZZA. One corner of the sign was flapping. The figure inside was wearing a branded windbreaker and was already leaning halfway out the window before the car had stopped.

lines.speaker: Tempo of the Rush Hour
lines.text: EVERYTHING! ALL OF IT! WHAT DO YOU MEAN WHAT?

lines.speaker: Tempo of the Rush Hour
lines.text: Irrelevant. You are on my route. You are on the clock. THE TIMER IS RUNNING. THE TIMER IS ALWAYS RUNNING.

steps.text: The compact car revved. It was not a powerful car. It was a car held together by determination and the belief that thirty minutes was a deadline and not a guideline. It accelerated toward them.

They ran. The car followed. Maple Heights was a maze of dead ends and looping streets that connected to other looping streets that also ended. Every time they turned a corner there was another corner. The Tempo was not deterred. The Tempo had a system.

lines.speaker: Tempo of the Rush Hour
lines.text: TWENTY-TWO MINUTES REMAINING! TWENTY-ONE! EVERY SECOND YOU WASTE IS A SECOND ON THE CLOCK!

steps.text: The Rogue found the skateboard leaning against a mailbox. It was purple. It had a cartoon flame on it. It would do.

The Rogue led the car down a footpath between two houses. Not a road. A path. The compact car followed anyway — the Tempo was past making distinctions at this point. The path ended at a fence. The fence was wooden and looked reasonable. The car hit it at a speed that suggested the Tempo had done the math and concluded the fence would yield. The fence did not yield. The fence had structural opinions.
The car stopped. The MAPLE HEIGHTS PIZZA sign was at an angle now. Steam came from somewhere under the hood that did not inspire confidence. The door opened.

lines.speaker: Tempo of the Rush Hour
lines.text: Eleven minutes. I still have eleven minutes.
